item.title: Healthcare gives Democrats a potent midterms attack line in Iowa and beyond
item.description: Shannon Gooden has voted Republican her entire life, including for Donald Trump three times. But the looming closure of the rural Iowa health clinic where she works has her questioning that loyalty ahead of November’s midterm elections.
item.content:encoded: Shannon Gooden has voted Republican her entire life, including for Donald Trump three times. But the looming closure of the rural Iowa health clinic where she works has her questioning that loyalty ahead of November’s midterm elections.

item.title: Big Tech data centers are driving up power bills at America's Rust Belt factories
item.description: For years, electricity costs for the Belden Brick Company in Sugarcreek, Ohio, had been relatively stable. Last year, they surged by 90% — largely because of rising power demand from data centers in the region.
item.content:encoded: For years, electricity costs for the Belden Brick Company in Sugarcreek, Ohio, had been relatively stable. Last year, they surged by 90% — largely because of rising power demand from data centers in the region.

item.title: Meta says US states are seeking $1.4 trillion in penalties in August youth safety trial
item.description: Meta Platforms said in a court filing on Monday that four states were seeking $1.4 ​trillion in penalties over accusations the company designed its Facebook and Instagram platforms to addict young users and misled the ‌public about their safety.
item.content:encoded: Meta Platforms said in a court filing on Monday that four states were seeking $1.4 ​trillion in penalties over accusations the company designed its Facebook and Instagram platforms to addict young users and misled the ‌public about their safety.

item.title: George E. Johnson, founder of pioneering Black hair products company, dead at 99
item.description: George E. Johnson, the pioneering Chicago entrepreneur whose eponymous company transformed Black hair care in the U.S. with brands ​including Afro Sheen, Ultra Wave and Classy Curl, died on Monday at age 99, according to news media ‌reports.
item.content:encoded: George E. Johnson, the pioneering Chicago entrepreneur whose eponymous company transformed Black hair care in the U.S. with brands ​including Afro Sheen, Ultra Wave and Classy Curl, died on Monday at age 99, according to news media ‌reports.

item.title: US makes available $281 mln in grants for addiction and mental health programs
item.description: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services said on Monday ​it is making available more ‌than $281 million in grant funding opportunities across 15 programs aimed at addressing addiction, ​overdose and mental illness.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services said on Monday ​it is making available more ‌than $281 million in grant funding opportunities across 15 programs aimed at addressing addiction, ​overdose and mental illness.

item.title: Democratic AGs oppose Trump plan to impose tariffs on forced labor concerns
item.description: A group of 22 Democratic state attorneys general on Monday opposed the Trump ​administration’s proposed tariffs of up to 12.5% on 59 countries and the European ‌Union over allegations they failed to curb trade in goods made with forced labor.
item.content:encoded: A group of 22 Democratic state attorneys general on Monday opposed the Trump ​administration’s proposed tariffs of up to 12.5% on 59 countries and the European ‌Union over allegations they failed to curb trade in goods made with forced labor.

item.title: Walmart rolls back barbecue staple prices after Trump says retailer acted at his request
item.description: Walmart said on Monday it would cut prices on many summer barbecue favorites, ‌including meat, chips, and soda, following a Truth Social post from President Donald Trump saying the retailer was doing so at his administration's request.
item.content:encoded: Walmart said on Monday it would cut prices on many summer barbecue favorites, ‌including meat, chips, and soda, following a Truth Social post from President Donald Trump saying the retailer was doing so at his administration's request.

item.title: Democratic Party leaders urge Platner to end Senate campaign amid sexual assault allegations
item.description: Democratic Party leaders on Monday called on Graham Platner to withdraw as their nominee to ​run against Republican U.S. Senator Susan Collins of Maine, after media reports that a woman accused him of forcibly having sex with her nearly five ‌years ago.
item.content:encoded: Democratic Party leaders on Monday called on Graham Platner to withdraw as their nominee to ​run against Republican U.S. Senator Susan Collins of Maine, after media reports that a woman accused him of forcibly having sex with her nearly five ‌years ago.

item.title: US cyber agency is using Anthropic's Mythos to audit government code
item.description: The ​U.S. cyber defense agency CISA is using Anthropic's AI model Mythos to audit government software, ‌three people familiar with the matter said on Monday, another sign of government enthusiasm for adopting the AI startup's tools even as the company navigates an ongoing standoff with the White House.
item.content:encoded: The ​U.S. cyber defense agency CISA is using Anthropic's AI model Mythos to audit government software, ‌three people familiar with the matter said on Monday, another sign of government enthusiasm for adopting the AI startup's tools even as the company navigates an ongoing standoff with the White House.

item.title: 'Accidental American' Balogun at crossroads of US, soccer politics at World Cup
item.description: Trump, who has repeatedly called for ending birthright citizenship, had publicly urged FIFA's president to review Balogun’s case. Balogun acquired U.S. citizenship by birth after his ​Nigerian mother was unable to return home to Britain from a trip because she was too far advanced in her pregnancy to fly.
item.content:encoded: Trump, who has repeatedly called for ending birthright citizenship, had publicly urged FIFA's president to review Balogun’s case. Balogun acquired U.S. citizenship by birth after his ​Nigerian mother was unable to return home to Britain from a trip because she was too far advanced in her pregnancy to fly.

item.title: US Supreme Court won't block Texas app store age verification law
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court declined on Monday to block a Texas law requiring app stores and developers to verify the age ​of mobile device users, and for minors to obtain parental consent, to download apps or make purchases, acting ‌in a challenge on free speech grounds by a technology industry group and students.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. Supreme Court declined on Monday to block a Texas law requiring app stores and developers to verify the age ​of mobile device users, and for minors to obtain parental consent, to download apps or make purchases, acting ‌in a challenge on free speech grounds by a technology industry group and students.

item.title: Tennessee Guard soldiers on Trump-backed task force kill armed man, authorities say
item.description: Two Tennessee National Guard soldiers assigned to a Donald Trump-backed anti-crime ​task force fatally shot an armed man being pursued by ‌Memphis police, authorities said, focusing attention on a high-profile federal crime-fighting initiative the president has touted as a success.
item.content:encoded: Two Tennessee National Guard soldiers assigned to a Donald Trump-backed anti-crime ​task force fatally shot an armed man being pursued by ‌Memphis police, authorities said, focusing attention on a high-profile federal crime-fighting initiative the president has touted as a success.

item.title: US service sector growth dips in June; employment rebounds after months of contraction
item.description: U.S. services sector activity dipped in June ​as some of the boost from businesses rushing to place orders amid the Middle East war ‌ebbed, but employment rebounded after contracting for three straight months, pointing to continued labor market stability.
item.content:encoded: U.S. services sector activity dipped in June ​as some of the boost from businesses rushing to place orders amid the Middle East war ‌ebbed, but employment rebounded after contracting for three straight months, pointing to continued labor market stability.

item.title: Trump says 500,000 children receive first $1,000 Trump account deposits
item.description: President Donald Trump said on Monday the government had deposited the first $1,000 into more than 500,000 "Trump Accounts," a program ​designed to give newborn Americans a stake in the stock market and ‌help build wealth from an early age.
item.content:encoded: President Donald Trump said on Monday the government had deposited the first $1,000 into more than 500,000 "Trump Accounts," a program ​designed to give newborn Americans a stake in the stock market and ‌help build wealth from an early age.

item.title: Utah investigator identifies Tyler Robinson as suspect in Charlie Kirk
item.description: A Utah state investigator on Monday identified Tyler Robinson in court as ​the suspect who video evidence showed was on a university campus four times the day prominent U.S. conservative activist Charlie Kirk was shot dead ‌while debating with students.
item.content:encoded: A Utah state investigator on Monday identified Tyler Robinson in court as ​the suspect who video evidence showed was on a university campus four times the day prominent U.S. conservative activist Charlie Kirk was shot dead ‌while debating with students.

item.title: Supreme Court ruling may wipe out Democrats' cash advantage in Senate battlegrounds
item.description: U.S. Senator Jon Ossoff of Georgia is a fundraising juggernaut, raising more than $81 million so far this cycle ​and holding nearly $33 million in campaign cash, or $30 million more than his opponent in the November election, Republican Representative Mike Collins.
item.content:encoded: U.S. Senator Jon Ossoff of Georgia is a fundraising juggernaut, raising more than $81 million so far this cycle ​and holding nearly $33 million in campaign cash, or $30 million more than his opponent in the November election, Republican Representative Mike Collins.

item.title: Ex-CIA chief Brennan, a Trump target, seeks to force DOJ to preserve records
item.description: Former CIA Director John Brennan sued on Wednesday seeking to force ‌the U.S. Justice Department to preserve records related to federal investigations examining his role in a U.S. intelligence community assessment that Russia boosted President Donald Trump's first presidential campaign in 2016.
item.content:encoded: Former CIA Director John Brennan sued on Wednesday seeking to force ‌the U.S. Justice Department to preserve records related to federal investigations examining his role in a U.S. intelligence community assessment that Russia boosted President Donald Trump's first presidential campaign in 2016.

item.title: Inglewood, once known for danger, uses World Cup to rebrand as a fun place
item.description: Sandra Lith came to the front of Ms. Ann's Boutique in Inglewood and asked a browsing World Cup visitor if he felt safe, if he had found people nice, and if ​he liked the small city in the Los Angeles area.
item.content:encoded: Sandra Lith came to the front of Ms. Ann's Boutique in Inglewood and asked a browsing World Cup visitor if he felt safe, if he had found people nice, and if ​he liked the small city in the Los Angeles area.

item.title: US designates Ecuador's Chone Killers gang a foreign terrorist group
item.description: The U.S. State Department said on Wednesday it had designated the ​Ecuadorean gang Chone Killers as a Foreign Terrorist Organization, ‌imposing sanctions on a group Washington accused of carrying out attacks on civilians and public officials.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. State Department said on Wednesday it had designated the ​Ecuadorean gang Chone Killers as a Foreign Terrorist Organization, ‌imposing sanctions on a group Washington accused of carrying out attacks on civilians and public officials.

item.title: US FTC says AI bias safeguards may run afoul of consumer law
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission on ​Wednesday said AI companies whose chatbots produce responses ‌that reflect "ideological objectives" may violate federal law, as part of a proposed policy on how the FTC will apply its authority ​to the sector.
item.content:encoded: The Federal Trade Commission on ​Wednesday said AI companies whose chatbots produce responses ‌that reflect "ideological objectives" may violate federal law, as part of a proposed policy on how the FTC will apply its authority ​to the sector.

item.title: Suspected member of “Scattered Spider” hacking group extradited to US from Finland, DOJ says
item.description: A 19-year-old man accused of being a member of the criminal hacking group "Scattered Spider" was extradited to the United ​States from Finland to face federal conspiracy charges in Illinois, the ‌Justice Department said on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded: A 19-year-old man accused of being a member of the criminal hacking group "Scattered Spider" was extradited to the United ​States from Finland to face federal conspiracy charges in Illinois, the ‌Justice Department said on Wednesday.

item.title: Russian daredevils unfurl banner atop New York's Empire State Building in proposal stunt
item.description: A Russian couple of daredevil artists climbed to the ‌top of the Empire State Building's towering spire in New York City on Wednesday to unfurl a large banner urging world peace in what appeared to be an elaborate marriage proposal that ended in their arrests.
item.content:encoded: A Russian couple of daredevil artists climbed to the ‌top of the Empire State Building's towering spire in New York City on Wednesday to unfurl a large banner urging world peace in what appeared to be an elaborate marriage proposal that ended in their arrests.

item.title: From Kansas to New York, historic heatwave bakes US ahead of July 4 holiday
item.description: Record-breaking ​temperatures spread to the eastern U.S. from the Midwest on Wednesday, putting tens of millions of people under heat warnings expected to last into the July 4 holiday ‌weekend, when Americans will celebrate the nation's 250th anniversary.
item.content:encoded: Record-breaking ​temperatures spread to the eastern U.S. from the Midwest on Wednesday, putting tens of millions of people under heat warnings expected to last into the July 4 holiday ‌weekend, when Americans will celebrate the nation's 250th anniversary.

item.title: Fed's Warsh says some task force staffing to be revealed next week
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh said on Wednesday that some of the key staffing of a ‌slate of “task forces” looking at central bank operations will be made public very soon and that non-Americans will be among these ranks.
item.content:encoded: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh said on Wednesday that some of the key staffing of a ‌slate of “task forces” looking at central bank operations will be made public very soon and that non-Americans will be among these ranks.

item.title: US declines to extend North American trade deal, starting clock to end it while seeking changes
item.description: The Trump administration on Wednesday declined to extend the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement, starting a decade-long clock to wind down the trade deal as it seeks changes to try ‌to reshore manufacturing jobs and reduce U.S. trade deficits with its North American neighbors.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration on Wednesday declined to extend the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement, starting a decade-long clock to wind down the trade deal as it seeks changes to try ‌to reshore manufacturing jobs and reduce U.S. trade deficits with its North American neighbors.

item.title: Trump urges acting spy chief to declassify records, including on 2020 election
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that his acting spy chief has wide permission to declassify records, ‌including any tied to the 2020 election, even though his close ally is only at the intelligence helm for a short time.
item.content:encoded: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that his acting spy chief has wide permission to declassify records, ‌including any tied to the 2020 election, even though his close ally is only at the intelligence helm for a short time.

item.title: Warnings galore as largest US power grid braces for record demand
item.description: PJM, the largest U.S. electric grid operator, on Wednesday detailed price spikes and warnings of massive transmission line congestion as it braced for record-breaking demand ​driven by a heatwave ahead of July 4 celebrations.
item.content:encoded: PJM, the largest U.S. electric grid operator, on Wednesday detailed price spikes and warnings of massive transmission line congestion as it braced for record-breaking demand ​driven by a heatwave ahead of July 4 celebrations.

item.title: House Republican leaders urge Trump to let Jones Act waivers expire as scheduled in August
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson and several Republican leaders are asking President Donald Trump ​to allow waivers to a law on shipping between domestic ports ‌to expire in mid-August as scheduled, rather than extending them further, according to a letter seen by Reuters.
item.content:encoded: House Speaker Mike Johnson and several Republican leaders are asking President Donald Trump ​to allow waivers to a law on shipping between domestic ports ‌to expire in mid-August as scheduled, rather than extending them further, according to a letter seen by Reuters.

item.title: Trump visits North Dakota for Roosevelt celebration, flies on new Air Force One
item.description: President Donald Trump rode a train through the Badlands of North Dakota on Wednesday to dedicate a museum honoring ​Theodore Roosevelt and invoke the Republican president's rugged legacy as part of celebrations for America's 250th anniversary.
item.content:encoded: President Donald Trump rode a train through the Badlands of North Dakota on Wednesday to dedicate a museum honoring ​Theodore Roosevelt and invoke the Republican president's rugged legacy as part of celebrations for America's 250th anniversary.

item.title: Transgender people finding it hard to win at Supreme Court
item.description: In May 1996, the U.S. Supreme Court struck down a voter-passed Colorado measure that had denied gay people legal protections against discrimination. It was a landmark ruling under the U.S. Constitution's promise of equal protection that heralded several other decisions by the court expanding LGBT rights in the decades to come.
item.content:encoded: In May 1996, the U.S. Supreme Court struck down a voter-passed Colorado measure that had denied gay people legal protections against discrimination. It was a landmark ruling under the U.S. Constitution's promise of equal protection that heralded several other decisions by the court expanding LGBT rights in the decades to come.

item.title: Despite some big losses, Supreme Court rewards Trump's assertion of power
item.description: On Tuesday, the U.S. Supreme Court ended Donald Trump's quest to narrow who can be considered a citizen if born in the United States, a harsh loss for the Republican president. But just one day before, it handed him a form of power that the dissenting justices said even English monarchs of the past did not possess.
item.content:encoded: On Tuesday, the U.S. Supreme Court ended Donald Trump's quest to narrow who can be considered a citizen if born in the United States, a harsh loss for the Republican president. But just one day before, it handed him a form of power that the dissenting justices said even English monarchs of the past did not possess.

item.title: Trump reports over $1.4 billion in income from crypto ventures
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump reported more than $1.4 billion in income from his family’s crypto ventures last year, showing how Trump now derives most of his income from digital assets that have benefited from his policies, ​according to a review of his latest financial disclosures on Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: U.S. President Donald Trump reported more than $1.4 billion in income from his family’s crypto ventures last year, showing how Trump now derives most of his income from digital assets that have benefited from his policies, ​according to a review of his latest financial disclosures on Tuesday.
